surface.name: Box Quick Start developer token and Get Current User via cURL
surface.selection_basis: Box Quick Start documents free developer signup, email confirmation, a pre-built application, and Generate Developer Token as the path to the first API call. The Developer Tokens guide shows the minimal authenticated call as curl GET https://api.box.com/2.0/users/me with Authorization: Bearer [DEVELOPER_TOKEN]. That cURL call is preferred over the Quick Start SDK upload-and-list tabs, which require a language SDK and a sample file.

alternatives_considered.name: Quick Start SDK upload file and list root folder
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Quick Start demonstrates upload plus get_folder_items / getFolderItems via Python, Node, Swift, or Java SDKs. Those tabs add SDK install and a sample PDF; the same developer token authenticates the lighter curl /users/me call documented on the Developer Tokens page.

alternatives_considered.name: Free Developer Plan default CCG app token exchange
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Free Developer Plan still documents an automatically created and authorized CCG app, but current Quick Start centers Generate Developer Token on the pre-built application rather than a CCG client-credentials exchange.

alternatives_considered.name: Postman or Box CLI quick starts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Those are separate tooling routes after the Quick Start foundations path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Box free developer account, generate a developer token from the pre-built app, and make a first authenticated API call without installing an SDK.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
The developer does not yet have a Box Free Developer account or a developer token.
A terminal with cURL is available; no language SDK is assumed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: After free developer signup, email confirmation, and Generate Developer Token on the pre-built application, an authenticated GET /users/me returns the logged-in console user.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: `curl https://api.box.com/2.0/users/me -H "authorization: Bearer [DEVELOPER_TOKEN]"` returns HTTP 200 and a user JSON object (fields such as `id`, `type`, `name`, and `login`).

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Quick Start frames the journey as sign up, generate the developer token, and make your first API call. The Developer Tokens guide demonstrates curl GET /users/me with the developer token in the Authorization header.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Quick Start defines signup, activation, pre-built app, and developer token. The Developer Tokens page supplies the shortest documented authenticated HTTP success with that token. An SDK upload-and-list is optional heavier tooling for the same credential.

prerequisites.requirement: A Box Free Developer account: name, email, password, terms acceptance, and Get Started on the developer signup page.
Email confirmation to activate the developer account, as stated on Quick Start.
A developer token generated from the pre-built application in the Developer Console. Tokens expire one hour after generation and are for development or testing only.
A terminal with cURL (or any HTTP client) able to send an authenticated HTTPS request. No language SDK is required for the compact first call.

primary_path.action: Open Box Quick Start and start free developer signup (Get started for free).
details: Quick Start states a free developer account gives access to the Developer Console, APIs, and AI Units.
primary_path.input: Intent to call the Box API
primary_path.success_signal: The signup form accepts name, email, and password.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Existing accounts use Log in instead of signup.

primary_path.action: Enter name and email, create a password, accept the terms, and select Get Started.
details: Free Developer Plan documents these signup fields and Get Started.
primary_path.success_signal: Signup completes and the developer is directed to activate or use the account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid email or rejected terms block completion.

primary_path.action: Confirm the email address to activate the developer account.
details: Quick Start: Confirm your email address to activate your developer account.
primary_path.input: Confirmation message delivered to the signup email
primary_path.success_signal: The developer account is activated and Developer Console access is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Delivery delay or missed confirmation leaves the account inactive per Quick Start.

primary_path.action: Open the Developer Console, select the pre-built application, and select Generate Developer Token under App Details / Developer Token.
details: Quick Start: a pre-built application is created for you; copy the generated token.
Developer Tokens guide: Configuration tab or My Platform Apps menu can also generate the token.
Developer tokens expire one hour after generation.
primary_path.success_signal: A developer token value is shown and can be copied.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No manual approval wait is documented for token generation on the free pre-built app.

primary_path.action: Call GET https://api.box.com/2.0/users/me with Authorization: Bearer [DEVELOPER_TOKEN] via cURL.
details: Example from the Developer Tokens guide: curl https://api.box.com/2.0/users/me -H "authorization: Bearer [DEVELOPER_TOKEN]"
primary_path.success_signal: HTTP 200 with a user JSON object for the console user associated with the token.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Expired, revoked, or missing tokens return auth errors; tokens cannot be refreshed programmatically.

primary_path.action: Confirm the response body identifies the authenticated user.
primary_path.success_signal: User fields such as id, type, name, and login are present in a successful response.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An error body means the first authenticated call did not succeed.

branches.condition: The developer follows a Quick Start SDK tab instead of cURL.
branches.path: Install the language SDK, initialize BoxClient with BoxDeveloperTokenAuth, upload the sample file to parent id 0, then list root folder items.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds SDK install and file upload before listing; the developer token credential is the same.

friction_gates.description: Quick Start requires email confirmation before the developer account is activated.
A developer token must be generated from the pre-built app; it expires after one hour and is not for production.

sources.title: Quick Start
sections_used: Sign up for a free account
Upload a file to Box and list all files in the root folder
Generate Developer Token on the pre-built application
evidence_supported: Pre-built application and Generate Developer Token
SDK upload-and-list tabs as heavier first-call demos

sources.title: Developer Tokens
evidence_supported: Generate Developer Token in Developer Console
curl GET /users/me with Bearer developer token
Token associated with the logged-in console user
Sixty-minute expiry and no programmatic refresh

sources.title: Free Developer Plan
sections_used: Sign up as a Box Developer
evidence_supported: Signup fields: name, email, password, terms, Get Started
Default CCG application note (not selected as primary token path)

uncertainties.question: Does Free Developer Plan 'activates immediately' still apply when Quick Start requires email confirmation?
uncertainties.impact: If confirmation is optional or already satisfied in some flows, step 3 may be skippable for some accounts.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Quick Start says confirm email to activate; Free Developer Plan says the account activates immediately. Both pages were current on 2026-07-22.

uncertainties.question: Is the automatically created free-account app labeled only as pre-built, or still described as the default CCG app in the console UI?
uncertainties.impact: Console copy may still mention CCG while Quick Start directs developers to Generate Developer Token.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Free Developer Plan documents automatic CCG app creation; Quick Start documents a pre-built application and developer token without requiring a CCG token exchange for first success.

excluded_after_success: SDK upload of demo-invoice and list root folder items
Create your first custom application after the pre-built app
Production OAuth, JWT, or CCG lifecycle
Postman and Box CLI tooling quick starts
App enablement, Integrations publication, webhooks, and enterprise rollout
